title: Kumbakkarai Falls
tagline: Kumbakkarai Falls — heritage city of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Kumbakkarai Falls are lesser known falls in the foothills of the Kodaikanal Hills.
overview.description: Kumbakkarai Falls are lesser known falls in the foothills of the Kodaikanal Hills. They are located at 10°10′48″N 77°31′50″E in Theni district, 9 kilometers (5.6 mi) from Periyakulam. Kumbakkarai Falls are located along the Kodai-Vellagavi-Periyakulam footpath. These falls have two stages.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Vadakarai
topPlaces.description: Vadakarai ('north bank') in Tharangambadi taluk, Theni district, Tamil Nadu, India, now forms part of that portion of Periyakulam municipality, which lies north of the Sankaraparani River. Still, it was once the chief village of a palaiyam of the same name.

topPlaces.name: Shembaganur Museum of Natural History
topPlaces.description: Shembaganur Museum of Natural History is a natural history museum located in the Kodaikanal region of Dindigul district, Tamil Nadu, South India. Established in 1895, the museum houses an extensive collection of exhibits across the fields of anthropology, botany, zoology, and handicrafts.

topPlaces.name: Periyakulam
topPlaces.description: Periyakulam (Tamil: [peɾijagɯɭam]) is a major town in Theni district, in the Madurai Region, South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. As of 2011, the town had a population of 42,976.

topPlaces.name: Silver Cascade Falls (India)
topPlaces.description: Silver Cascade Falls is a waterfall located in Kodaikanal, Dindigul district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Palani Hills
topPlaces.description: The Palani Hills are a mountain range in the southern Indian states of Tamil Nadu and Kerala. The Palani Hills are an eastward extension of the Western Ghats ranges, which run parallel to the west coast of India.

topPlaces.name: Kodaikanal Lake
topPlaces.description: Kodaikanal Lake (Tamil: கொடைக்கானல் ஏரி), also known as Kodai Lake, is a manmade lake located in the Kodaikanal city in Dindigul district in Tamil Nadu, India. Sir Vere Henry Levinge, the then Collector of Madurai, was instrumental in creating the lake in 1863, amidst the Kodaikanal town which was developed by the British and early missionaries from USA. The lake is said to be Kodaikanal's most popular geographic landmark and tourist attraction.

topPlaces.name: Kodaikanal block
topPlaces.description: Kodaikanal block is a revenue block in the Dindigul district of Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 15 panchayat villages.

topPlaces.name: Kodaikanal division
topPlaces.description: Kodaikanal division is a revenue division in the Dindigul district of Tamil Nadu, India.

items.note: Vadakarai ('north bank') in Tharangambadi taluk, Theni district, Tamil Nadu, India, now forms part of that portion of Periyakulam municipality, which lies north of the Sankaraparani River. Still, it was once the chief village of a palaiyam of the same name.

Shembaganur Museum of Natural History is a natural history museum located in the Kodaikanal region of Dindigul district, Tamil Nadu, South India. Established in 1895, the museum houses an extensive collection of exhibits across the fields of anthropology, botany, zoology, and handicrafts.
Periyakulam (Tamil: [peɾijagɯɭam]) is a major town in Theni district, in the Madurai Region, South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. As of 2011, the town had a population of 42,976.
Silver Cascade Falls is a waterfall located in Kodaikanal, Dindigul district, Tamil Nadu, India.

The Palani Hills are a mountain range in the southern Indian states of Tamil Nadu and Kerala. The Palani Hills are an eastward extension of the Western Ghats ranges, which run parallel to the west coast of India.

Kodaikanal Lake (Tamil: கொடைக்கானல் ஏரி), also known as Kodai Lake, is a manmade lake located in the Kodaikanal city in Dindigul district in Tamil Nadu, India. Sir Vere Henry Levinge, the then Collector of Madurai, was instrumental in creating the lake in 1863, amidst the Kodaikanal town which was developed by the British and early missionaries from USA. The lake is said to be Kodaikanal's most popular geographic landmark and tourist attraction.
Kodaikanal block is a revenue block in the Dindigul district of Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 15 panchayat villages.
Kodaikanal division is a revenue division in the Dindigul district of Tamil Nadu, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kumbakkarai Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kumbakkarai Falls is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kumbakkarai Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (73 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (71 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kumbakkarai Falls?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kumbakkarai Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kumbakkarai Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Kumbakkarai Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kumbakkarai Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vadakarai, Shembaganur Museum of Natural History, Periyakulam, Silver Cascade Falls (India), Palani Hills.
